query: Agentcard: gives your AI agent its own debit card: fund a wallet, set a budget, and it issues single-use cards review

results.title: OpenAI wants your launches
results.content:
So we teamed up with OpenAI to put credits behind it. Build something with Sol, Terra, or Luna, schedule your launch for Thursday, July 23, and the top products walk away with $10k in OpenAI credits.

If you're going to kick the tires anyway, might as well get paid for it. You've got nine days: schedule your launch for July 23.

## Cards that die on purpose

Agentcard gives your AI agent its own debit card: fund a wallet, set a budget, and it issues single-use cards that close themselves the moment a charge clears. Karen Serfaty (@keyserfaty) was running Atlas when Remote acquired it, then turned down a job at Stripe to build this instead. [...] 🔥 Our Take: Visa and Mastercard are both teaching agents to spend, top down, with specs and pilot programs. Agentcard shipped the bottom-up version: a card scoped to one purchase, dead the second it clears, so your agent can only ever spend exactly what you approved. Nobody is worried their agent can't check out. Everyone is worried about what else it might check out. One commenter watched their assistant buy a domain end to end and called it "kind of wild." The wild part wears off. The leash is what you're paying for.

## Grow your app with Setapp: revenue, users, & AI

Newsletter Sponsor

You shipped the app. Now comes the part nobody warns you about. [...] newsletter icon

July 14th, 2026

# OpenAI wants your launches

## OpenAI wants your launches

Today: an AI agent gets its own debit card, a guard dog moves in with your database, and your trackpad learns to breathe. Plus $10k in OpenAI credits for whatever you build next.

## We teamed up with OpenAI

OpenAI just dropped GPT-5.6, their largest model yet, and it's the first model in a while that has a lot of us opening a different terminal: trying a new harness, moving workflows over, comparing outputs. The labs leapfrogging each other is how everyone building on top ends up with better tools.

Younes Alturkey 🐟

#### What's great

The single-use, charge-on-spend model is a smart take on the trust problem with agent payments — scoping each card to one purchase neatly caps the blast radius if an agent misbehaves. Also a good call making it MCP-compatible out of the gate; that's the right bet for where agent tooling is heading. Congrats on the launch! 🚀 [...] Agentcard
